title: Bonafousia killipii
taxonomy: Bonafousia killipii is a species within the family of flowering plants known as Apocynaceae. This family is a large group of plants that are commonly known as dogbane or oleander. Apocynaceae is further classified within the order Gentianales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Asclepiadaceae and Loganiaceae. The order Gentianales is part of the clade Asterids, a large group of flowering plants that also includes the orders Lamiales and Solanales. The clade Asterids is part of the clade Angiosperms, which is the largest and most diverse group of flowering plants.

intro: Bonafousia killipii, also known as the Killip's Bonafousia or Killip's plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Bonafousia killipii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health complaints, including fever, colds, and respiratory infections.
